puzzle.title: The Adopted Soul's Journey
puzzle.intro: In a time when societal norms held sway, a unique individual experienced a profound transformation within a blessed household. Their story, marked by a bond of affection and a divine decree, illustrates the breaking of old traditions and the establishment of new, righteous principles. This narrative highlights the importance of truth over convention and the wisdom behind Allah's commands.
clues: I was once a captive, but through divine arrangement, I found a new home and family.
I was so beloved that when given a choice between my biological family and my adopted guardian, I chose to remain with the latter.
My name was once associated with my adopted guardian, a practice that was common in my society.
I am the only companion of the final Prophet whose name is explicitly mentioned by Allah in the Quran.
I married a noble woman who was a cousin of the Prophet, but our marriage did not last.
After my divorce, Allah Himself decreed that my former wife would marry the Prophet, thereby nullifying an ancient custom regarding adoption.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Leading an army at a young age
The divine decree regarding my marriage and divorce
trial.answer: The divine decree regarding my marriage and divorce

location.label: Key Element
options: The custom of adoption and its implications
location.answer: The custom of adoption and its implications

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Becoming the first muezzin of Islam
The abolition of the pre-Islamic practice of 'calling adopted sons as biological sons'
The appointment of a successor to the Prophet
outcome.answer: The abolition of the pre-Islamic practice of 'calling adopted sons as biological sons'

puzzle.arabic: وَإِذْ تَقُولُ لِلَّذِىٓ أَنْعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَأَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيْهِ أَمْسِكْ عَلَيْكَ زَوْجَكَ وَٱتَّقِ ٱللَّهَ وَتُخْفِى فِى نَفْسِكَ مَا ٱللَّهُ مُبْدِيهِ وَتَخْشَى ٱلنَّاسَ وَٱللَّهُ أَحَقُّ أَن تَخْشَىٰهُ ۖ فَلَمَّا قَضَىٰ زَيْدٌۭ مِّنْهَا وَطَرًۭا زَوَّجْنَـٰكَهَا لِكَىْ لَا يَكُونَ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ حَرَجٌۭ فِىٓ أَزْوَٰجِ أَدْعِيَآئِهِمْ إِذَا قَضَوْا۟ مِنْهُنَّ وَطَرًۭا ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرُ ٱللَّهِ مَفْعُولًۭا
puzzle.verse: And [remember, O Muḥammad], when you said to the one on whom Allāh bestowed favor and you bestowed favor, "Keep your wife and fear Allāh," while you concealed within yourself that which Allāh is to disclose. And you feared the people, while Allāh has more right that you fear Him. So when Zayd had no longer any need for her, We married her to you in order that there not be upon the believers any discomfort [i.e., guilt] concerning the wives of their claimed [i.e., adopted] sons when they no longer have need of them. And ever is the command [i.e., decree] of Allāh accomplished. (33:37)
